title: Dashboard: dependency graph view (server-rendered SVG)
description: Add a fourth Graph view to corvee render alongside Board / Tree / Summary, visualizing the cross-cutting Dependencies and Blocks relations as a DAG. Constraints from CLAUDE.md §9 rule out client-side libs (Mermaid, D3, vis-network all add 500KB+ bundles) and shelling out to Graphviz at render time (adds runtime dep). The right path is server-side SVG, computed in Go: topological layer assignment (longest path from any source), within-layer ordering by ID for determinism, nodes as rounded rects colored by status, edges as right-pointing Bezier arrows. For workspaces beyond ~200 nodes will need a focus-on-item-plus-neighbors filter — dovetails with the existing right-side drawer (click node opens drawer; drawer dep link re-focuses graph). Today the same data is reachable via 'corvee export --format dot | dot -Tsvg' for users with graphviz installed; this story adds the in-dashboard view for users without that tooling.
acceptance_criteria: Server-side layered DAG layout in Go — no third-party JS/CSS deps
Click node opens the existing right-side drawer with item details
Output deterministic across runs (snapshot-testable like the other views)
Performance acceptable up to 500 nodes; beyond that
